evidence: MusiteDeep is a deep-learning predictor for protein PTM site prediction; it operates entirely within this task definition (per-residue binary classification per PTM type using a sequence window)

MusiteDeep's timestamp-based benchmark on 13 PTM types shows higher AUC and area-under-PR than ModPred (logistic regression) and the strongest classical per-PTM baseline (DeepPhos, GlycoEP, GPS-PAIL, MePred-RF, CSS-Palm4.0, UbiProber, GPS-SUMO, RF-Hydroxysite) on 12 of 13 PTM types under both metrics
Review's central organizing concept; the paper defines and frames multi-omics integration as joint ML+network-pharmacology pipeline.
Synthesis review collates qualitative case-study evidence that multi-omics + ML + network-pharmacology pipelines recover signals beyond single-omics analysis.
Paper coins the term 'Posttranslational Modification Inspired Drug Design (PTMI-DD)' and lays out its four operational strategies (covalent / induced-pocket / PPI / degrader).

Paper presents asciminib (ABL001) myristoyl-pocket allosteric Bcr-Abl inhibition, tazemetostat EZH2 inhibition, HDAC inhibitor approvals, thalidomide/CRBN degraders, and a self-reported covalent RhoA inhibitor as evidence that targeting PTM-modified isoforms yields better selectivity and expands the druggable space.
Atz et al. (2021) provide the canonical molecular-sciences review of GDL, defining the term and organizing the field around symmetry priors.
The review surveys SE(3)/E(3)-equivariant message-passing GNNs (TFN, EGNN, Cormorant, NequIP, PaiNN, SE(3)-Transformer) as a central GDL family for molecular modelling.
The review covers RNN- and transformer-based chemical language models on SMILES (and SELFIES, DeepSMILES) as one of the four representation classes of molecular GDL.

Review synthesizes evidence from quantum-chemistry, drug-discovery, and protein-structure-prediction literature that geometric priors improve molecular modelling, while documenting the inconsistent gain in drug discovery.
Review explicitly documents reliable equivariant-GNN gains for quantum chemistry but inconsistent gains for drug-discovery property prediction.

Review centers the E3 ubiquitin ligase concept across cancer; consolidates cancer-relevant E3 families (HECT, RING, RBR; CRLs incl SCF-FBXW7, SCF-SKP2, CRL3-SPOP, CRL2-VHL; APC/C; MDM2; BRCA1-BARD1; parkin; NEDD4).
Review treats ubiquitylation cascade (E1-E2-E3, chain topology K48/K63/mono-Ub) as the underlying PTM driving cancer-relevant E3 function.

Primary supporting source for the claim; consolidates mouse-genetic, biochemical and clinical evidence that E3 deregulation drives cancer phenotypes via altered substrate stability and is therapeutically tractable in a context-dependent manner.
UbiBrowser is the canonical naive-Bayes ESI-prediction platform that defines the modern formulation of this concept (golden standards, five evidence channels, pair-input split protocol).
Five-fold CV AUROC 0.827, independent test set AUROC 0.73, three published case validations and one wet-lab Smurf1-Smad3 confirmation.
Paper uses FoldDock pipeline (AF2 + paired MSA + chain-break) to predict 65,484 human PPI complexes.
Uses pDockQ as the interface-confidence score for ranking AF2-predicted complexes; calibrates pDockQ thresholds against 1,465 solved complexes.
Operationalizes interface residues from predicted structures to map disease mutations and phosphosites at human-interactome scale.
Predicts 65,484 human PPI complexes; 3,137 high-confidence (pDockQ > 0.5) with 80% accuracy on solved structures and 75% XL-MS support.
Paper is the canonical 2024 description of the AlphaFold DB resource; it details data archiving, access channels, and integration.
Paper details PAE schema, storage format (JSON), and improvements to the PAE viewer (non-consecutive region highlighting and Mol* coupling).
Paper codifies the operational pLDDT tier guidance (>90/70-90/50-70/<50) and the storage convention (B-factor field, _ma_qa_metric_local) used across AFDB consumers.
Paper reports scaling AFDB from 360k to 214M+ structures and integration into 7+ primary biological databases — the canonical evidence for the claim.
Paper documents both metrics as first-class outputs and invests engineering effort specifically in coupling the PAE viewer to the 3D viewer for inter-domain interpretation.
Releases CCSB-HI1: 2,754 binary human PPIs over 1,549 proteins; the dataset name is coined and its protocol fully defined in this paper.

Adopts and stringently re-engineers the yeast two-hybrid system (Gateway-cloned ORFeome, 96-well mating, 3 reporter genes, plasmid-shuffling auto-activator counter-selection) as the primary high-throughput PPI-detection assay.

Operationalizes 'human interactome' as the set of binary PPIs detectable in at least one exogenous assay; explicitly excludes complex membership and dynamics. Treats the map as a binary-edge scaffold.

Provides primary evidence: 424 disease-associated edges of which 352 are previously unliterature-mentioned, with co-AP verification rate ~78% on Y2H-only edges, plus enrichment for shared GO / motif / mouse-phenotype / coexpression.
AlphaFold2 introduces and names the Evoformer block as the trunk of the network.
AlphaFold2 introduces invariant point attention as the SE(3)-invariant attention mechanism inside the structure module.
AlphaFold2 introduces FAPE as the primary structure-prediction loss.

AlphaFold2 reports CASP14 median backbone Calpha r.m.s.d.95 of 0.96A and a 1.46A median full-chain accuracy on post-cutoff PDB chains, supporting the claim that deep learning attains near-experimental atomic accuracy from sequence.
Figure 5a shows accuracy collapses below median Neff ~30 and saturates above ~100, with the threshold pattern present in both high- and low-template-coverage subgroups.
AlphaFold 3 introduces the pairformer as the dominant trunk block, replacing the AF2 evoformer; 48 blocks operate on pair and single representations only.
AlphaFold 3 introduces a diffusion module operating on raw atomic coordinates that replaces the AF2 frame-based structure module.
AF3 instantiates a single deep-learning model that predicts protein, nucleic-acid, ligand, ion, and modified-residue complexes in one unified framework.
AF3 head-to-head benchmark wins versus specialised baselines across protein-ligand, protein-nucleic-acid, protein-protein, antibody-antigen, and protein-monomer evaluations.
AF3 replaces AF2 IPA with an unconstrained diffusion module yet improves protein-monomer LDDT (P=1.74e-34) and exceeds equivariant generalists like RoseTTAFold All-Atom on PoseBusters (P=4.45e-25).

AF3 directly extends and reworks the AF2 architecture: pairformer replaces evoformer; diffusion module replaces frame-based structure module; uses AF2-Multimer cross-distillation to suppress hallucination.
AF3 reports significantly higher protein-monomer LDDT than AF2/AF-Multimer 2.3 (P = 1.74e-34) on the same evaluation regime.
Generated by ideate; targets weakly_supported claim
PTM-isoform degraders rely on E3-substrate axis being context-dependent
Inspired by PTMI-DD framing
E3-substrate prior from UbiBrowser
Boltz-2 / AF3 supply PTM-occupied POI structures
PTM-conditioned ensemble may strengthen or contradict the diffusion-without-equivariance claim
MSA-orthogonal pair-rep scaling tests whether MSA depth alone bounds ensemble recovery
AF3-phospho collapse motivates the work
Builds on diffusion structure-prediction backbone
Pair-representation scaling adapter sits on the Evoformer/Pairformer pair representation
PTM-conditional interactome nominates druggable PPI hotspots
Extends AF2-Multimer interactome with PTM stratification
Direct extension of FoldDock pipeline + pDockQ
Binary edges from CCSB-HI1 / HuRI scaffold
Reuses FoldDock pipeline
ΔpDockQ-per-PTM is the headline operator

Stage 1 baseline reproduction; planned. Reproduces DeepTernary published values on TernaryDB CRBN+VHL test split as a precondition for ΔpTernary computation.
Stage 2b primary validation; planned. Tests whether calibrated ΔpTernary ranking on phospho-PROTAC held-out track lifts AUC by >= 0.05 over PTM-blind baseline.
Stage 3 ablation; planned. Tests scorer-robustness: whether the ΔpTernary AUC lift survives when DeepTernary is replaced with PROTAC-STAN.
Stage 4 robustness; planned. Tests whether the phospho-PROTAC AUC lift generalizes to ubiquitylation- and methylation-degron tracks.
Stage 4 robustness; planned. Tests the mutant-isoform PROTAC track (p53-Y220C, R175H) separately from the PTM track to prevent conflation in the headline number.

Stage 3 route-comparison ablation; planned. Tests whether MD-relaxed phospho-structures yield ΔpTernary scores with Pearson r >= 0.7 and |disagreement| < 0.10 vs Boltz-2 native CCD-PTM-token predictions on a matched 25-tuple subset.

Stage 2a load-bearing fail-fast gate; planned. Characterises per-POI null distribution of ΔpTernary under random size-matched POI surface perturbations on TernaryDB CRBN+VHL training subset; gates the entire downstream pipeline.
Stage 3 calibration ablation; planned. Isolates whether replacing the per-POI noise-floor calibration with raw |ΔpTernary| reduces top-K=20 ranking AUC by >= 0.03.
release alongside DeepTernary, Nat. Commun. 2025; CRBN+VHL test split — the exact split reproduced by this experiment
Paper surveys E3 ubiquitin ligases as oncogenic drivers and therapeutic targets across multiple cancers; explicitly proposes targeting these proteins via PROTAC/small-molecule modulators.
E3 ligases enzymatically catalyze the transfer of ubiquitin onto substrate proteins (= the ubiquitylation reaction). Canonical mechanism.

PROTAC-class degraders (the paradigm described in posttranslational-modification-inspired-drug-design) bind E3 ubiquitin ligases — predominantly CRBN, VHL, MDM2, IAP family — via dedicated E3-recruitment ligands, which is the load-bearing molecular event in the ternary-complex mechanism.
MusiteDeep is trained and benchmarked on UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot human PTM annotations across 8 PTM types (phosphorylation, ubiquitination, acetylation, methylation, …).
Phase-0 noise-floor calibration runs on the CRBN+VHL training subset of TernaryDB v1.

Specific CRBN-recruitment ligands (IMiD glutarimide handle — thalidomide / lenalidomide / pomalidomide) are the canonical anchor for CRBN-based PROTAC and molecular-glue degraders described in this drug-design concept; this is the more-specific instance of the class-level concepts/posttranslational-modification-inspired-drug-design --binds--> concepts/ubiquitin-ligase-e3 edge.
